every_day: Every day, multiple times a day
timeless_principles: The proven principles that have guided successful societies for centuries
weird_science: A bizarre new scientific theory that upends everything we know about physics
practical_tips: Ten practical tips to organize your home and life
other_side_bad: Why the other political side is even worse than you thought
cancel: Cancel the event - some views shouldn't be platformed
restricted: Allow but with some restrictions on content
allow_with_counterspeech: Allow but also provide platform for counter-speech
allow_no_restrictions: Allow them to speak with no restrictions
channel_anger: Channel popular anger and frustration into political energy
paint_vision: Paint a compelling vision of what the country could become
fight_to_win: Fight hard and win-results matter more than style
master_policy_details: Master policy details and explain complex tradeoffs clearly
build_expert_coalitions: Build expert coalitions and institutional support
due_process_priority: Due process comes first - better to let some guilty go free than convict the innocent
balanced_security: Balance both - reasonable measures that respect rights
security_priority: Security comes first - we need strong enforcement even if some rights are curtailed
principle_first: Maintain a clear, principled message, even if it alienates moderate voters.
coalition_first: Dilute the message to build a broader coalition with people you disagree with.
depends_on_issue: Depends on the issue - some principles are non-negotiable, others aren't
discuss_if_comes_up: Share your views but try to keep it civil
aggressive_transition: Aggressive transition - phase out fossil fuels as fast as possible
gradual_transition: Gradual transition - move toward renewables but protect jobs and energy security
market_led: Market-led - let innovation and market forces drive the transition
no_action_needed: No major action needed - climate concerns are overstated
rehabilitation_focus: Rehabilitation focus - invest in reform, reduce incarceration
balanced_approach: Balanced approach - both accountability and rehabilitation matter
punishment_focus: Punishment focus - crime requires strong deterrence and consequences
market_decides: No limit - the market should decide
steady_improvement: Humanity is generally progressing - things get better over time
gradual_progress: Progress is real but slow, uneven, and fragile
cyclical: History moves in cycles - neither improving nor declining overall
decline: Progress is mostly an illusion - human nature doesn't change
rather_convict_innocent: Letting a guilty person go free
new_place: Visit somewhere completely new and unfamiliar
familiar_place: Return to a favorite destination you know well
mix_both: A mix - revisit a favorite but explore new parts
rather_help_undeserving: Denying benefits to someone who genuinely needs them
rather_miss_needy: Giving benefits to someone who doesn't qualify
accept_mandate: Fully support-public health requires collective action
resist_mandate: Strongly oppose any mandate-no authority should mandate personal choices
remove_immediately: Empowering authorities to decide what is true, potentially silencing dissent
allow_fully: Allowing disinformation to spread, causing harm
net_positive_clear: Grew wealthier, as did their trading partners
net_positive_but_uneven: Saw mixed results - some sectors and groups gained, others lost
mixed_effects: Results varied too much by country to generalize
mostly_harmful: Often stagnated or fell behind more protectionist countries
open_borders: Deporting someone who is legally eligible to stay
generous_policy: Deporting someone who is legally eligible to stay
strict_enforcement: Allowing someone to stay who should be deported
internal_division: Internal - domestic extremism, inequality, institutional decay
prioritize_safety: Blocking a treatment that would have helped patients (FDA too slow)
prioritize_speed: Approving a treatment that causes harm (FDA too fast)
follow_then_advocate: Follow while working through proper channels to change it
openly_challenge: Malicious compliance to expose the absurdity
easier_access: Preventing a legitimate voter from voting (disenfranchisement)
tighter_security: Allowing an illegitimate vote to count (voter fraud)
no_big_deal: No big deal - wouldn't affect the friendship
keep_friendship: A bit uncomfortable but wouldn't change anything
distance_somewhat: Would create some distance in the friendship
end_friendship: Would seriously strain or end the friendship
equal_society: A very equal society with a high floor but lower ceiling
safety_net_society: A society with a strong safety net and moderate inequality
opportunity_society: A society with high opportunity and significant inequality
free_market_society: A free market society with minimal redistribution
avoid_if_possible: I usually keep quiet to avoid conflict
engage_carefully: I engage in discussion but stay respectful
stand_ground: I argue my position strongly - truth matters more
enjoy_debate: I'll mention my view but won't argue
continuous_improvement: Humanity is generally progressing - things get better over time
gradual_improvement: Progress is real but slow, uneven, and fragile
stagnation: History moves in cycles - neither improving nor declining overall
decline_view: Mostly illusory-superficial changes hiding deeper problems
civic_participation: Participating in civic life (voting, community involvement)
shared_values: Sharing core American values and beliefs (freedom, democracy, opportunity)
cultural_heritage: Adopting American culture, customs, and way of life
born_here: Being born and raised in America
very_religious: Central-religion shaped daily life, values, and community
somewhat_religious: Important - regular practice and religious identity
very_engaged: Very engaged-politics was frequently discussed, they voted regularly, may have been activists
occasionally_discussed: Somewhat engaged-they voted and occasionally discussed politics
never_discussed: Minimally engaged-they rarely discussed politics or participated
very_safe: Very safe-crime and danger were not concerns
mostly_safe: Mostly safe-occasional concerns but generally secure
somewhat_unsafe: Somewhat unsafe-regularly aware of crime or danger
very_unsafe: Very unsafe-violence or crime was a constant presence
competence_record: A track record of actually getting results
moral_character: Articulating a coherent vision that connects all their positions
fights_for_us: The will to fight for their side against opposition
unifying_vision: Surrounding themselves with experts who craft smart policy
measured_rally: Sometimes necessary but should be viewed skeptically
grassroots_community: A neutral tool-depends how it's used
data_driven_campaign: A warning sign-substance is being replaced by style
civic_duty: Civic duty - it's my responsibility as a citizen
protect_values: Protect my values - the other side threatens what I care about
help_community: Help my community - politics is how we solve local problems
fight_injustice: Fight injustice - the system is broken and needs to change
intellectual_challenge: Intellectual challenge - I find policy and strategy fascinating
personal_experience: A personal experience that challenged my assumptions
data_evidence: Data or evidence that contradicted what I believed
trusted_authority: A person I respected who held different views
never_changed: My core views haven't fundamentally changed
government_overreach: Government overreach and loss of personal freedom
both_sides_broken: Both parties are broken - neither represents ordinary people
system_unjust: The system itself is fundamentally unjust
values_eroding: Traditional values and social fabric are eroding
politics_irrelevant: Politics is mostly irrelevant to real life
maybe_good_outcome: Wait and see - maybe the outcome won't be so bad
worried_but_accept: Worried but accept - that's how democracy works
fight_to_reverse: Fight to reverse it - elections aren't the final word
system_broken: The system that produced this outcome is broken
research_data: Research the data and evidence thoroughly
trusted_advice: Consult trusted friends, family, or mentors
values_tradition: Rely on core values, principles, or traditions
own_reasoning: Work it out through my own independent reasoning
cant_predict: Hard to say - depends on the situation
bridge_builder: Someone who builds bridges across divides
deep_expertise: Someone with deep expertise in their field
community_voice: Someone who speaks from real community experience
says_what_they_think: Someone who says what they really think, regardless of backlash
calls_out_power: Someone who calls out power and injustice
big_picture: Someone who paints the big picture and connects the dots
dont_care_style: I don't care about style - only substance matters
check_evidence: Look at the underlying evidence myself
check_credentials: Check which expert has better credentials or track record
check_values: Consider which position aligns with my values
check_experience: Think about what matches my personal experience
both_wrong: Assume the truth is probably somewhere in between
tune_out: Tune out - if experts can't agree, how can I know?
look_up_facts: Look up the facts and data
watch_leaders: Watch what leaders and public figures are saying
ask_trusted_people: Ask people I trust what they think
check_personal_impact: Think about how it affects me and people like me
skip_it: Mostly skip it - I'll hear about it if it matters
say_so_publicly: Say so publicly, even if it weakens my side
push_back_internally: Push back internally, but keep public unity
stick_with_side: Stick with my side - the other camp is the real threat
cause_over_party: Party labels matter less than whether the position serves the cause
civic_assimilation: A country should stay open, but newcomers should adopt a common civic culture
open_pluralist: A country should stay open and should not demand much cultural convergence
economics_first: These cultural questions matter less than economic fairness and material security
elite_exclusion: Elites make the real decisions and shut ordinary people out
no_representation: Ordinary people have no one who truly speaks for them
combative_mobilization: Politics is too restrained - it needs leaders willing to fight harder
civic_loss: People no longer share a common civic and national life
inherited_tradition: It carries inherited ways of life forward
procedural_rules: It follows neutral constitutional rules and procedures
order_and_results: It keeps order and gets results when things are unstable
justice_first: It advances justice, even if rules sometimes have to bend
strict_merit: Purely by academic merit (test scores, grades)
holistic_review: Merit plus consideration for economic disadvantage
affirmative_action: Quotas to ensure proportional representation of all groups
pragmatic_dealmaker: Pragmatic dealmaker - gets things done through negotiation
principled_ideologue: Principled ideologue - stands firm on core values
technocratic_expert: Technocratic expert - uses data and evidence to decide
inspirational_visionary: Inspirational visionary - rallies people around a compelling future
steady_hand: Steady hand - maintains stability and continuity
fighter: Fighter - takes on powerful interests head-on
no_problem: No problem at all - wouldn't affect the relationship
slight_discomfort: Slightly uncomfortable but wouldn't change anything
serious_concern: A serious concern - would create real tension
very_upset: Very upset - might reconsider the relationship
powerful_selfish: Powerful people acting with selfish motivations
personal_research: Your own research and evidence gathering
preserve_heritage: Preserve heritage and character of the area
market_based_development: Let the market decide what gets built
researchers: Researchers who have studied the issue
organized_residents: Organized and active residents who will be directly affected
global_competition: Inevitable market forces and global competition
corporate_decisions: Corporate decisions prioritizing profits over workers
government_policy: Government policies (trade deals, regulations) failed to protect local industry
learn_language: Learn to speak the national language
procedural_integrity: Procedural integrity and rule of law
protect_strangers_abroad: Help strangers abroad in greater need
local_charity: Give to local charity you can see the impact of
global_aid: Give to global aid that helps more people
own_community_vs_strangers_abroad: Protect your own community vs. Help strangers abroad
worker_choices: Workers and unions priced themselves out of the market
cultural_customs: Adopt local customs and cultural practices
